message:
refactor(cli): unify last-touched-id tracking at caller site, simplify flush paths

Refactor how mutation commands track the "last touched" issue ID, which
is used by subsequent commands (e.g. \`br close\` followed by \`br reopen\`
with no explicit ID) to default to the most recently modified issue.

Previously, each command had two divergent code paths for setting the
last-touched ID:
1. Inline within the mutation loop (only when using a database backend)
2. Inside a \`flush_no_db_then\` closure (only for JSONL/no-db mode)

This dual tracking was error-prone and meant that cross-project routing
could not reliably set last-touched in the *invoking* workspace (it was
set in the *target* workspace instead). The fix moves set_last_touched_id
to the top-level execute function, after all routes have been processed,
so it always writes to the invoking workspace's beads_dir.

Affected commands:
- **close**: Remove inline per-issue tracking and flush_no_db_then
  closure; set last-touched once from closed_issues.last() at caller.
- **defer/undefer**: Same pattern -- remove inline tracking and
  flush_no_db_then closures; set last-touched at caller for both
  defer and undefer paths.
- **reopen**: Same pattern as close/defer.
- **label add/remove**: Move last-touched from flush_no_db_then into
  the routing aggregation site so it uses the invoking workspace.
- **update**: Return resolved_ids from execute_prepared_route so the
  caller can set last-touched. Also avoids redundant get_all_ids()
  calls by threading the ID list through resolve_target_ids and
  resolve_parent_update.
- **comments add**: Replace flush_no_db_then with flush_no_db_if_dirty
  and set last-touched unconditionally afterward.
- **dep add/remove**: Remove redundant rebuild_blocked_cache(true)
  calls that duplicated work already done by the storage layer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
